description: Combines the exact finite-state automata calculations of Dančík and Paterson with the theoretical scaling laws of the Kardar-Parisi-Zhang (KPZ) universality class to extrapolate infinite-limit bounds.
analogical_connections: Like using thermodynamic equations of state to predict the asymptotic limit of a microscopic fluid simulation.
implementation_hypothesis: Extend the Feasible Triplet method to sequence lengths n up to 10^5, fit the KPZ finite-size scaling exponent \beta=1/3 to the empirical data, and infer the offset \gamma_2 with rigorous error bounds.
experiment_seed: Simulate LCS for length n=1000..50000. Regress E[L_n] = \gamma_2 n + A n^{1/3} + B. Extract \gamma_2 and test tightness.

description: Frames the LCS alignment problem as a variant of the Push Totally Asymmetric Simple Exclusion Process (PushTASEP), mapping the growth rate of the LCS to the steady-state particle current.
analogical_connections: Traffic flow optimization (maximizing throughput) corresponds to maximizing the shared subsequence between random environments.
implementation_hypothesis: Identify the exact invariant measure for the frog dynamics model on a finite ring, compute the macroscopic current, and extrapolate as ring size approaches infinity.
experiment_seed: Simulate frog dynamics (Bukh & Cox 2022) for small N, track particle current J, and derive its exact generating function.

description: Treats the discrete LCS matrix as a discretized PDE. Derives a macroscopic differential equation for the 'average speed' of optimal alignments using calculus of variations.
analogical_connections: Similar to how geometric optics approximates Maxwell's equations; continuous ray-tracing approximates discrete dynamic programming paths.
implementation_hypothesis: Formulate a continuous control problem whose objective is bounded by Vachkovskaia's thermodynamic limits, and use finite element methods to establish a strict upper bound.
experiment_seed: Solve the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation corresponding to the fluid limit of the LCS path ensemble.

description: Adapts the replica symmetry cavity method from spin glass theory to formulate a local message-passing algorithm that computes marginal probabilities of character alignments.
analogical_connections: LCS is analogous to the ground state energy of a disordered 2D lattice. Message passing computes the free energy.
implementation_hypothesis: Implement Belief Propagation on the bipartite alignment graph of two random sequences. Use the Density Evolution framework to track message distributions and compute the expected asymptotic free energy.
experiment_seed: Run Belief Propagation on random pairs of strings of length N=1000. Compare the zero-temperature limit of free energy with known lower bounds.

description: Combines Kingman's subadditive ergodic theorem with exponential tilting (large deviation theory) to drastically sharpen the concentration inequalities used in branch-and-bound lower bounds.
analogical_connections: Like using importance sampling to find rare events in financial risk modeling, tilted martingales zoom in on the 'typical' high-yield alignments.
implementation_hypothesis: Modify Lueker's branch-and-bound exploration using a tilted probability measure that favors sequences with higher local LCS growth, re-weighting to establish a rigorous lower bound.
experiment_seed: Compute the moment generating function of finite blocks L_k (k=10) and apply Cramér's theorem to bound the tail of L_n.

description: Models the DP matrix anti-diagonals as a fluid density profile undergoing Burgers' equation. The LCS constant is bounded by the speed of the macroscopic shockwave.
mathematical_formalization: \partial_t u + u \partial_x u = \nu \partial_{xx} u, \text{ speed } s = \frac{u_L + u_R}{2}
analogical_connections: The 'front' of completed LCS computations propagates through the DP grid like a shockwave through a viscous fluid.
implementation_hypothesis: Map the DP grid transitions to microscopic collision rules, derive the macroscopic Riemann problem, and extract the wave speed.
experiment_seed: Track the expected maximum value along anti-diagonals k=1..2n. Measure the linear propagation speed.

description: Relates the fluctuations of the LCS length to the Tracy-Widom distribution and the largest eigenvalue of the GUE random matrix ensemble.
analogical_connections: The maximum alignment score maps to the ground state energy of a system of non-intersecting fermions.
implementation_hypothesis: Use exact Fredholm determinant representations of the TASEP transition probabilities to compute the exact coefficients of the n^{1/3} expansion, tightly constraining \gamma_2.
experiment_seed: Generate 10,000 pairs of n=5000. Fit the empirical distribution of L_n to the Tracy-Widom F_2 distribution and solve for \gamma_2 and c.

description: Formulates the step-by-step pairing of characters as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) and uses Reinforcement Learning to discover near-optimal local alignment policies that establish rigorous expected lower bounds.
analogical_connections: Just as AlphaGo finds lower bounds on the win-rate of board positions through self-play, an RL agent finds lower bounds on the LCS growth rate.
implementation_hypothesis: Train a Deep Q-Network on a finite lookahead window of the two random strings. Prove the policy is stationary and its average reward per step strictly lower-bounds \gamma_2.
experiment_seed: Train PPO with a lookahead of 15 characters to choose DP moves. Evaluate expected reward per step.

description: Represents the dynamic programming alignment matrix as a 2D tensor network, reducing the computation of the expected exponential partition function to Matrix Product State (MPS) compression.
analogical_connections: Compressing the ensemble of all possible string alignments is equivalent to finding the low-entanglement ground state of a 1D quantum chain.
implementation_hypothesis: Construct the transfer matrix for the finite-temperature LCS problem. Use Density Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG) to find the leading eigenvalue.
experiment_seed: Implement a tensor network with bond dimension \chi=16. Compute \lambda_{\max} for varying temperatures \beta and extrapolate to \beta \to \infty.

description: Examines the hypothesis that \gamma_2 is an exact algebraic number root of a polynomial system (as originally posited then retracted by Tiskin), mapping the failures of exact solvability to chaotic dynamics.
mathematical_formalization: P(\gamma_2) = 0 \text{ for } P \in \mathbb{Z}[x] \text{ vs } \lambda \text{ is transcendental}
analogical_connections: Similar to how the three-body problem fails to have a closed-form algebraic integral of motion, the infinite-state automaton of LCS may resist exact algebraic closure.
implementation_hypothesis: Formulate a finite algebraic relaxation using Lasserre (Sum of Squares) hierarchy. Track the degree at which the algebraic closure fails to accurately represent the infinite limit.
experiment_seed: Construct the transition polynomials for k-state approximations. Check Gröbner bases for exact rational/algebraic roots.
